title: witness-mcdc/v1
description: MC/DC report — per-decision truth tables, condition-level independent-effect verdicts under masking MC/DC (DO-178C accepted variant), and row-closure recommendations for conditions that lack a proving pair. Derived from witness-core/src/mcdc_report.rs (McdcReport).

schema.description: Schema URL identifying this document. Always equal to the URL `https://pulseengine.eu/witness-mcdc/v1`.

witness_version.description: Version of the witness toolchain that produced this report (Cargo package version).

module.description: Path of the instrumented Wasm module the report was produced from. May be project-relative when SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH and path normalisation are active.

interpretation_polarity.description: v0.10.0 — names the polarity convention the truth-table cells use. Always `wasm-early-exit` in v0.10.x: `c_i=true` means "the wasm br_if value at condition i was true," which inverts source-level `c_i` for any condition lowered through `i32.eqz`. Lets viewers explain the `(year % 4 == 0) && ...` row vector at the right level of abstraction. v0.10.x may add `source-equivalent` as an opt-in. Omitted on v0.9.x reports.

McdcOverall.description: Aggregate counts across the run. Boolean-decision counts (decisions_total, decisions_full_mcdc) exclude br_table-shape decisions because per-arm coverage is the honest measurement for those (see v0.9.7 br-table-arm interpretation).

source_file.description: Source file (DWARF) the decision was reconstructed from. Absent when DWARF source location is unavailable.

source_line.description: 1-based source line number the decision starts on. Absent when DWARF source location is unavailable.

truth_table.description: Read-only view of every observed condition vector + outcome for this decision.

br_table_audit.description: v0.11.5 — discriminant-bit MC/DC audit verdict for br_table-shape decisions. Present only when (a) the decision is br_table-shaped, (b) at least one row carried `raw_brvals` data (v0.11.5+ instrumentation). The per-condition `conditions` list above stays the headline reviewer view (per-arm coverage); this block carries the textbook MC/DC-over-the-discriminant proof that DO-178C objective 5.2 work needs.

bit_width.description: Number of discriminant bits the audit considered. Equals the position of the highest set bit across all observed discriminants, plus one (capped to 32, the wasm i32 width).

bits.description: Per-bit verdict in ascending bit-position order (bit 0 first).

status.description:
Aggregate audit status.
- `proved` — every bit position has an independent-effect witness pair.
- `partial` — some bits proved, some have gaps.
- `gap` — at least one observed bit has no proving pair.
- `not_applicable` — decision was reached but no rows carried raw_brvals (pre-v0.11.5 instrumentation).

Per-bit verdict.
- `proved` — found a row pair where this bit differs and the firing arm differs.
- `gap` — bit was observed but no proving pair.
- `dead` — every observed discriminant agreed on this bit's value.

pair.description: Row pair witnessing independent effect of this bit. Present only when `status == proved`.

DecisionStatus.description:
Decision-level verdict.
- `full_mcdc` — every condition has an independent-effect witness pair.
- `partial` — some conditions are proved, others have gaps or are dead.
- `no_witness` — decision was reached but no conditions could be proved (e.g. only one row, or all rows have the same condition vector).
- `unreached` — decision had zero rows in the run.

ConditionVerdict.description: Verdict for one condition within a decision.

branch_id.description: Manifest branch id this condition corresponds to.

interpretation.description:
Pair interpretation when status is `proved`. Values:
- `unique-cause` — both rows of the proving pair fully evaluated every condition (no short-circuiting).
- `masking` — at least one row of the pair short-circuited a non-target condition (DO-178C masking variant).
- `unique-cause-plus-masking` — reserved for the in-between case (currently unused; kept for forward compatibility).
- `br-table-arm` — v0.9.7 — decision is br_table-shaped (mutually exclusive arms). Each condition is an arm and `proved` means the arm hit count is > 0; standard MC/DC pair-finding does not apply.

pair.description: Row ids of the proving pair when status is `proved`. Absent when no pair was found.

gap_closure.description: Recommended row to close the missing pair when status is `gap`. Absent for `proved` and `dead`.

ConditionStatus.description:
Condition-level verdict.
- `proved` — independent effect demonstrated by an existing row pair.
- `gap` — condition was evaluated in at least one row but no proving pair exists; an additional test row would close it.
- `dead` — condition was never evaluated across any row in the run (no test exercises it).

GapClosure.description: Recommendation for closing a `gap`-status condition by flipping the target value relative to an existing row.

paired_with_row.description: Row id this recommendation pairs against.

evaluated.description: Recommended condition vector (target condition flipped from paired_with_row's value, others identical). Object whose keys are stringified u32 condition indices and values are booleans.

outcome_must_differ_from.description: Outcome of paired_with_row. The recommended row's outcome must differ from this value to close the pair under masking MC/DC.

RowView.description: One observation of a decision's condition vector under short-circuit evaluation.

row_id.description: Stable row identifier carried back in the proving-pair tuple.

evaluated.description: Sparse map of condition_index (stringified) -> evaluated boolean. Conditions short-circuited in this row are absent.

outcome.description: Decision outcome when observed. `null` when the decision was reached but the boolean result was not observed (e.g. function panicked or returned mid-chain).

TraceHealth.description: Health of the per-row capture path. Reporters MUST refuse to emit MC/DC verdicts when `overflow` is true.

overflow.description: True if any per-condition counter saturated between row markers (degenerate condition: a row evaluated the same condition more than once).

rows.description: Total rows captured across all decisions.

trace_parser_active.description: v0.10.0 — renamed from `ambiguous_rows` (the legacy field name was misleading: it described whether the trace-buffer parser produced per-iteration rows, not whether outcomes were ambiguous). True when post-v0.7.3 instrumentation produced kind=0/kind=2 trace records the parser could decode into rows. Pre-v0.7.3 modules and harness-v1 snapshots leave this `false` (only counter aggregates). Witness still accepts the legacy `ambiguous_rows` name on input via serde alias; producers emit only `trace_parser_active`.

ambiguous_rows.description: Deprecated v0.9.x alias of `trace_parser_active`. Witness deserialises this name for back-compat with v0.9.x run-record loads but never emits it; consumers SHOULD prefer `trace_parser_active`.

bytes_used.description: v0.9.8 — total trace memory bytes consumed across all rows.

pages_allocated.description: v0.9.8 — number of Wasm memory pages the instrumented module allocates for its trace buffer (default 16 = 1 MiB).
